summary: README is excellent — clear motivation, quick start, design rationale, platform guidance, and community fork links. program.md is an outstanding AI agent instruction file — potentially the most notable extractable artifact. Missing: LICENSE (critical), CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, CHANGELOG, API docs. Inline code documentation is minimal but the code is self-documenting given its simplicity.

findings.detail: Comprehensive README with: vision statement, how-it-works section, quick start (4 commands), project structure, design choices with rationale, platform support with workarounds for smaller hardware, notable forks section.

Exceptional AI agent instruction file. Covers: setup protocol (6 steps), experimentation rules (CAN/CANNOT), output format parsing, TSV logging format, experiment loop (keep/discard), crash recovery, timeout handling, NEVER STOP autonomy instruction. This is the key extractable pattern for JASON-OS.

Minimal docstrings — a few comments in train.py for model architecture. prepare.py has a module docstring and function docstrings. Code is largely self-documenting due to clear naming (GPTConfig, CausalSelfAttention, evaluate_bpb).
No LICENSE (critical blocker), no CONTRIBUTING.md, no SECURITY.md, no CHANGELOG, no architecture docs beyond README. Community health score 28/100.
Good: fixed time budget, uv.lock for reproducible deps, .python-version file, clear setup instructions. Gap: no Docker/container setup, results depend on GPU hardware.
